commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `/btw` to select the configured harness/runtime, resolve models through `resolveModelAsync` with runtime-aware static-catalog fallback and discovery retry, and adds regression tests for canonical aliases routed through CLI runtimes.

PR surface: Source +80, Tests +68. Total +148 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with high confidence: current `/btw` performs a single-provider registry lookup that cannot resolve the reported canonical alias when it is absent from discovered models. The linked issue also provides a deterministic released-version setup, though this review did not execute that live environment.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A feature-history owner should confirm the shared transport-ownership contract, and the contributor must provide real after-fix runtime proof before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): align /btw model resolution with runtime aliases
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92168.

`/btw` used a registry-only model lookup, while the main agent loop resolves models through `resolveModelAsync` with runtime-aware static catalog fallback. That made `/btw` fail for canonical provider aliases such as `anthropic/*` when they are intentionally routed through a CLI runtime via `agentRuntime.id` and no direct provider entry exists in the agent's discovered `models.json`.

This PR aligns `/btw` model resolution with the main agent path by:

- resolving the selected harness/runtime before model lookup
- using `resolveModelAsync` instead of the primitive registry-only resolver
- allowing bundled static catalog fallback when a plugin/CLI runtime owns transport
- preserving the existing discovered-models retry path for normal OpenClaw-owned transports

## Tests

- `npm test -- --run src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- `npm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- `npm run tsgo:core:test`
- `npx oxfmt --check src/agents/btw.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- `npx oxlint src/agents/btw.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `npm run check:changed` could not run locally because the configured Blacksmith/crabbox wrapper failed its own binary sanity check before executing repository checks.
target.author: wangwllu
